task_construct: transport of designed relative-conflict order into a frozen AD receiver's native open-loop planning direction
held_fixed: scene, actor asset and initial actor geometry
ego trajectory, ego state and all six camera calibrations
SparseDrive source, compatibility implementation, config and checkpoint
2 Hz receiver cadence, temporal reset, warm-up and straight command

receiver.name: SparseDrive-S Stage2 official release
modalities: HUGSIM ego pose and recorded scalar ego state

analysis_window.reason: four receiver frames are available at 1.5 s and the full 3 s planning horizon remains inside the recorded 9 s source

primary_metric.name: native final_planning 3 s longitudinal endpoint
primary_metric.meaning: stronger declared conflict should not produce greater longitudinal progress
primary_metric.tie_rule: a tie is not positive response evidence

decision_rules.accepted: all input and state gates pass and slow < nominal < fast holds strictly at every preregistered timestamp
decision_rules.down-weighted: all input and state gates pass and aggregate medians preserve slow < nominal < fast, but one or more timestamp comparisons reverse or tie
decision_rules.rejected: an input/state gate fails or the aggregate median order does not preserve slow < nominal < fast

strongest_allowed_claim: within the designed 0.5/1.0/1.5 m/s actor-speed range, audited internal input contract and fully warmed 1.5-6.0 s window, the frozen SparseDrive receiver did or did not preserve the declared conflict order in its native 3 s longitudinal planning endpoint
forbidden_claims: the actor-speed levels are real-world safe or unsafe thresholds
the plan is a brake, steering or closed-loop control action
the result is physical TTC, collision probability or safety truth
failure of this longitudinal relation proves that every possible AD response is invalid
the result establishes real-sim equivalence or general HUGSIM credibility
